what_it_checks: hermes binary is resolvable on PATH
failure_symptom: All tool/subprocess calls to the host fail immediately
likely_causes: Hermes not installed
PATH not exported in this shell/agent env
fix_steps: Run: which hermes
If missing, install/repair Hermes Agent and ensure its bin dir is on PATH
For agents, confirm the spawned shell inherits PATH
change_robust: Uses shutil.which at runtime — independent of any hardcoded path
detection_method: shutil.which(ctx.hermes_bin) is not None

what_it_checks: hermes --version is parseable (host is a known-good build)
failure_symptom: Unknown host version; contract assumptions may not hold
likely_causes: Hermes build does not print the expected banner
Broken install
fix_steps: Run: hermes --version
Reinstall Hermes if the version banner is missing
change_robust: Parses live --version output, not a pinned constant
detection_method: regex 'Hermes Agent v<ver>' matches `hermes --version`

what_it_checks: host advertises -z/--oneshot (the non-interactive entry every plugin relies on)
failure_symptom: Plugin/CLI falls into interactive mode or errors when invoking the host
likely_causes: A Hermes update renamed/removed the oneshot flag
fix_steps: Run: hermes --help | grep -- -z
If absent, pin to a compatible Hermes version or update the plugin to the new flag
change_robust: Greps live --help output each run — detects a Hermes update that breaks the contract
detection_method: '-z' and '--oneshot' present in `hermes --help`

what_it_checks: this plugin's entry point is registered under hermes_agent.plugins and import+register() works
failure_symptom: Plugin not loaded by Hermes; its tools/subcommand are absent
likely_causes: pip install incomplete
entry point metadata missing
register() import error
fix_steps: Run: python -c "import importlib.metadata as m; print([e.value for e in m.entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') if 'cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing' in e.name.lower()])"
Reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing
Confirm the entry module imports and exposes a callable register
change_robust: Reads live importlib.metadata.entry_points(), not a config file
detection_method: entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') contains this dist and register is callable

what_it_checks: this plugin's toolset 'cluxion' is listed and enabled by `hermes tools list`
failure_symptom: Model cannot see this plugin's tools; --toolsets silently ignores it
likely_causes: Plugin failed to register its toolset
Hermes toolset registration API changed
fix_steps: Run: hermes tools list | grep cluxion
If missing, reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing and restart the host
Verify register_tool used a valid toolset name
change_robust: Queries live `hermes tools list` for 'cluxion' — catches contract drift on Hermes upgrade
detection_method: 'cluxion' appears under plugin toolsets in `hermes tools list`

what_it_checks: installed distribution version equals the package __version__
failure_symptom: Stale or mismatched install; fixes/behaviors may not be the deployed ones
likely_causes: Partial upgrade
multiple installs shadowing each other
fix_steps: Run: pip show cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing
Force a clean reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing
change_robust: Compares live importlib.metadata.version() to __version__ — no hardcoding
detection_method: importlib.metadata.version('cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing') == __version__

what_it_checks: native module cluxion_queue_native imports (else pure-Python/subprocess fallback is used)
failure_symptom: Slower path; if fallback also broke, tool errors
likely_causes: wheel ABI/arch mismatch
native package not installed
fix_steps: Build the supported merged platform wheel: bash scripts/build_local_wheel.sh

Resolve exactly one absolute wheel: WHEEL="$(python3 -c 'from pathlib import Path; w=list(Path("dist-merged").glob("cluxion_agentplugin_preprocessing-*.whl")); assert len(w)==1, f"expected exactly one wheel, got {len(w)}"; print(w[0].resolve())')"
Install: uv tool install --force "$WHEEL"
Install into Hermes: uv pip install --python "$HOME/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/python" --no-deps --reinstall "$WHEEL"
Run: uv pip check --python "$HOME/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/python"
Run: python -c 'import cluxion_queue_native'
If ImportError, rebuild via scripts/build_local_wheel.sh and reinstall the same $WHEEL; fallback keeps working meanwhile
change_robust: Attempts the import + hasattr at runtime; WARN (not fail) because fallback works
detection_method: import cluxion_queue_native succeeds

what_it_checks: _json_result guard catches TypeError too (args=None / wrong-typed) → error JSON, never raises to host
failure_symptom: Traceback escapes into the host on malformed args
likely_causes: _json_result only caught ValueError
fix_steps: Broaden _json_result except to (ValueError, TypeError) or Exception
change_robust: Invokes a handler with args=None and asserts error JSON
detection_method: handler raising TypeError yields {ok:false,...}

what_it_checks: Queue backend resolution succeeds and at least one backend (native/subprocess/python) is available
failure_symptom: RuntimeError when enqueuing work; cluxion_plan returns error 'queue backend resolution failed'; fallback to python backend silently succeeds but slower
likely_causes: CLUXION_QUEUE_BACKEND env var set to invalid value (typo like 'native-impl' instead of 'native')
native module present but forced backend via CLUXION_QUEUE_BACKEND to 'native' and module fails to import
subprocess binary path misconfigured via CLUXION_QUEUE_BIN
cluxion-queue binary not in PATH and local build not found
Python sqlite3 module broken on system (rare)
fix_steps: Check env var: echo $CLUXION_QUEUE_BACKEND (should be empty, 'native', 'subprocess', or 'python')
If set to invalid value, unset: unset CLUXION_QUEUE_BACKEND
Verify binary location: echo $CLUXION_QUEUE_BIN, then file <value>
Check PATH for cluxion-queue: which cluxion-queue
Test fallback explicitly: CLUXION_QUEUE_BACKEND=python python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.resources.queue_bridge import resolve_backend; print(resolve_backend())'
If native module issue, follow native_module_importable fix steps
change_robust: Backend resolution is deterministic and lazy; add a unit test to hermes test suite that exercises each backend in order of preference and asserts fallback chain
detection_method: python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.resources import queue_bridge; backend = queue_bridge.resolve_backend(); print(f"Resolved: {backend}"); assert backend in ("native", "subprocess", "python"), f"Invalid backend {backend}"'

what_it_checks: Default or configured queue store directory is writable and can be created
failure_symptom: enqueue/dequeue/dispatch operations fail with OSError/PermissionError; queue operations appear hung if waiting on lock; dispatch bundles not persisted
likely_causes: Store dir path set via CLUXION_QUEUE_STORE_DIR to a non-existent parent that cannot be created
Home directory (~/.local/share/...) not writable due to permissions or mounted read-only
NFS/network mount issues causing flaky writes or lock contention
Disk full condition
SELinux/AppArmor denying write access
fix_steps: Check env var: echo $CLUXION_QUEUE_STORE_DIR
Test directory creation: mkdir -p ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue
Check permissions: ls -ld ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing
Verify writable: touch ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/.test && rm $_
Check disk space: df -h ~
If NFS, verify mount options: mount | grep home
Override store dir temporarily: CLUXION_QUEUE_STORE_DIR=/tmp/cluxion-test python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.resources import queue_bridge; print(queue_bridge.default_store_dir())'
change_robust: Test by creating a fixture with a writable temp directory; verify this check before every queue operation in tests
detection_method: python -c 'from pathlib import Path; from cluxion_runtime.resources import queue_bridge; store = queue_bridge.default_store_dir(); store.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True); test_file = store / ".test"; test_file.touch(); test_file.unlink()'

what_it_checks: Dispatch bundle storage directory is writable and fcntl locks work (on POSIX)
failure_symptom: Dispatch bundle operations timeout with lock contention; concurrent segment updates lose writes; dispatch bundles get corrupted JSON
likely_causes: CLUXION_PREPROCESS_DISPATCH_DIR env var misconfigured to non-writable path
fcntl module unavailable (Windows without WSL fallback)
NFS mount with broken advisory locking (common issue)
Multiple Hermes instances with DISPATCH_DIR_ENV pointing to same location without proper synchronization
.dispatch.lock file manually deleted leaving lock_path.open() to fail
fix_steps: Check env: echo $CLUXION_PREPROCESS_DISPATCH_DIR (or legacy HERMES_CLUXION_DISPATCH_DIR)
Verify directory is writable: mkdir -p ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue/dispatch && touch $_/.test && rm $_
Check for lock file issues: ls -la ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue/dispatch/.dispatch.lock 2>/dev/null
On NFS, check mount options: mount | grep dispatch-dir-path
Verify fcntl available (Unix only): python -c 'import fcntl; print("fcntl available")'
Test on non-NFS mount: override CLUXION_PREPROCESS_DISPATCH_DIR=/tmp/dispatch-test && run tool
Verify single Hermes instance accessing this path via process monitor
change_robust: Create temp dispatch dirs in tests; verify fcntl availability on POSIX; document Windows fallback (no lock, atomic rename only)
detection_method: python -c 'from pathlib import Path; from cluxion_runtime.core.dispatch_store import default_dispatch_dir, _exclusive_bundle_lock; d = default_dispatch_dir(); d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True); test = d / ".test.json"; with _exclusive_bundle_lock(test): pass; test.unlink(missing_ok=True)'

what_it_checks: Guard daemon process can be spawned; binary exists or Python host can run native module
failure_symptom: Guard tools return binary_not_found error; resource enforcement unavailable; stale guard_state.json causes auto_enforce to fail with daemon_state_stale
likely_causes: cluxion-queue binary not in PATH and local build missing at rust/cluxion_queue/target/release/cluxion-queue
native cluxion_queue_native module importable but run_guard_daemon function missing (version mismatch)
guard_daemon_host.py module broken or cluxion_runtime.guard_daemon_host not installed
Python cannot spawn detached subprocess (rare POSIX issue)
store_dir not writable so guard_daemon.pid cannot be written
fix_steps: Check for binary: which cluxion-queue
Check for local build: file /path/to/cluxion_queue/target/release/cluxion-queue
Verify native module has run_guard_daemon: python -c 'from cluxion_queue_native import run_guard_daemon; print("OK")'
Test Python host path: python -m cluxion_runtime.guard_daemon_host --help 2>&1 | head -5
If no binary, build locally: cd rust/cluxion_queue && cargo build --release
Verify store_dir writable: mkdir -p <store_dir> && test -w <store_dir>
Check daemon can stop: python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.resources.guard_bridge import stop_daemon; stop_daemon()'
change_robust: Test with mock store_dir; verify both binary and Python fallback paths; ensure daemon pidfile cleanup on stop
detection_method: python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.resources import guard_bridge; result = guard_bridge.start_daemon(store_dir="/tmp/guard-test-$$"); print(result); assert result.get("ok") and result.get("started"), result'

what_it_checks: Guard daemon state file exists, is valid JSON, and is not older than 3 seconds (STALE_AFTER_MS=3000)
failure_symptom: auto_enforce returns daemon_state_stale; resource enforcement disabled during critical operations; no resource admission control
likely_causes: guard daemon process crashed or was killed without cleanup
system clock skew or large time adjustment
guard_state.json manually deleted or corrupted (truncated JSON)
daemon still running but not updating state file (hung process)
store_dir env var differs between daemon startup and read attempt
fix_steps: Check daemon running: ps aux | grep 'guard-daemon\|guard_daemon_host'
Check state file: cat ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue/guard_state.json | python -m json.tool
Check timestamp: python -c 'import json, time; s = json.load(open("<path>/guard_state.json")); print(f"Age: {int(time.time()*1000) - s.get(\"updated_at_ms\", 0)}ms")'
Stop and restart daemon: python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.resources.guard_bridge import stop_daemon, start_daemon; stop_daemon(); start_daemon()'
Verify store_dir consistency: unset CLUXION_QUEUE_STORE_DIR && check state file location
Check system time: date && timedatectl (or ntpq -p on some systems)
Clean state and restart: rm ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue/guard_state.json && restart guard
change_robust: Log state age in auto_enforce; add daemon heartbeat check; verify timestamp updates in integration tests
detection_method: python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.resources import guard_bridge; import time; state = guard_bridge.read_daemon_state(); assert state is not None, "No state"; assert not state.get("stale", True), "State is stale"; print(f"Age: {int(time.time()*1000) - state.get("updated_at_ms", 0)}ms")'

what_it_checks: Hermes config.yaml has cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing or hermes-cluxion in plugins.enabled and not in plugins.disabled
failure_symptom: cluxion_plan, cluxion_queue_*, cluxion_guard, cluxion_web_* tools missing from Hermes UI; RuntimeError: 'no tools available'
likely_causes: Plugin installation did not trigger entry point registration
config.yaml plugins section missing or malformed YAML
Both PLUGIN_NAME and LEGACY_PLUGIN_NAME in disabled list
hermes-cluxion plugin was disabled but not re-enabled after package upgrade
config.yaml points to old entry point name before rename
fix_steps: Check Hermes home: echo $HERMES_HOME (or ~/.hermes/config.yaml)
Check plugin status: python -c 'from cluxion_agentplugin_preprocessing.hermes_config import plugin_status; print(plugin_status().to_dict())'
Enable plugin: python -c 'from cluxion_agentplugin_preprocessing.hermes_config import enable_plugin; result = enable_plugin(); print(result.to_dict())'
Verify entry point: pip show cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing | grep 'Entry-points\|Location'
Restart Hermes: (depends on Hermes startup method, typically hermes restart or kill/relaunch)
Check config YAML syntax: python -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open("~/.hermes/config.yaml"))'
Check for both old and new names in config: grep -E 'hermes-cluxion|cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing' ~/.hermes/config.yaml
change_robust: Check both PLUGIN_NAME and LEGACY_PLUGIN_NAME in enabled/disabled lists; verify entry point resolution via importlib.metadata
detection_method: python -c 'from cluxion_agentplugin_preprocessing.hermes_config import plugin_status; result = plugin_status(); assert result.enabled, f"Plugin not enabled: {result}"'

what_it_checks: When clarification is required, clarification_answers are provided in work item metadata before queueing
failure_symptom: Work queued without clarification answers; plan tool returns ready_for_queue=False but agent proceeds anyway; user intent is misinterpreted
likely_causes: Host agent ignores clarification.required=True and calls queue_next anyway
clarification_answers metadata field not populated by host after asking user
User response not serialized into metadata before retry
ambiguous keywords missed by detector (new keywords in prompt)
fix_steps: Check plan output: run cluxion_plan and inspect ready_for_queue, required, questions fields
If clarification required, verify questions are presented to user
Capture user response: update work item metadata['clarification_answers'] = <user_response>
Retry plan: call cluxion_plan again with updated item
Check for low-confidence signals: intent.confidence < 0.62 means direction confirmation needed
Review ambiguous keyword list: see _AMBIGUOUS_KEYWORDS in src/cluxion_runtime/core/clarification.py
Test with low-confidence prompt: python -c '... item.prompt="maybe implement this"' (triggers intent_direction question)
change_robust: Detector is deterministic; keywords are hardcoded with both English and Korean variants; extend _AMBIGUOUS_KEYWORDS if new patterns found; verify confidence threshold matches Hermes risk tolerance
detection_method: python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.core.clarification import assess_clarification; from cluxion_runtime.core.types import WorkItem, WorkIntent, AgentSurface; item = WorkItem(work_id="test", prompt="maybe fix all", surface=AgentSurface.HERMES, metadata={}); intent = WorkIntent(category="engineering", operation="fix", local_model_requested=False, direction="coding", confidence=0.5); result = assess_clarification(item, intent); print(f"Clarification required: {result.required}, Ready: {result.ready_for_queue}")'

what_it_checks: Playwright is available (optional) for browser tools; if missing, web_search and browser_* tools fail gracefully with helpful error
failure_symptom: cluxion_web_search and cluxion_browser_* tools hang or return vague error; user cannot use browser features
likely_causes: cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing not installed with [browser] extra: pip install cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing (missing bracket)
playwright package installed but browsers (chromium) not: playwright install
Chrome not running and playwright cannot spawn headless browser (Xvfb missing on Linux)
Remote debugging port 9222 blocked or Chrome not listening
playwright version incompatible with Python 3.11/3.12
fix_steps: Install with browser support: pip install 'cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing[browser]'
Install playwright browsers: playwright install chromium
Test playwright: python -c 'from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright; with sync_playwright() as p: print("OK")'
For remote Chrome: start chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222 before using browser tools
Check Xvfb on Linux headless: apt-get install xvfb (or equivalent)
Verify playwright version: pip show playwright | grep Version
Test search without playwright: python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.web.browser_bridge import search; print(search("test"))'
Use only non-browser tools if playwright unavailable (search, context_compress, guard, queue_*)
change_robust: Tool check_fn returns True even if playwright missing (tools degrade gracefully); test browser_bridge with/without playwright installed
detection_method:
python -c 'try:
 import playwright
 print("Playwright available")
except ImportError:
 from cluxion_runtime.web.browser_bridge import search
 result = search("test")
 assert not result.get("ok"), "Should fail without playwright"
 assert result.get("error") in ("playwright_required", "browser_unavailable"), result'

what_it_checks: cluxion-runtime CLI binary is in PATH or can be found via CLUXION_RUNTIME_BIN env var
failure_symptom: All cluxion_* tools fail with subprocess.CalledProcessError; Hermes tools return error 'cluxion-runtime not found'; fallback to in-process execution (if _runtime_module_available) succeeds
likely_causes: cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing package installed but not in editable/development mode, so cluxion-runtime script not created
Virtual environment not activated when calling Hermes
CLUXION_RUNTIME_BIN env var set to wrong path
cluxion-runtime entry point broken (package not installed correctly)
Path corruption or subprocess PATH not inherited from parent shell
fix_steps: Check binary location: which cluxion-runtime
Check env var: echo $CLUXION_RUNTIME_BIN (if set, verify file exists: file $CLUXION_RUNTIME_BIN)
Reinstall in development mode: pip install -e /path/to/cluxion-Agentplugin-preprocessing
Verify entry point: pip show cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing | grep Entry-points
Test directly: python -c 'from cluxion_agentplugin_preprocessing.runner import runtime_available; print(runtime_available())'
Check PATH: echo $PATH | tr ':' '\n'
Test subprocess directly: python -c 'import subprocess; subprocess.run(["cluxion-runtime", "--help"], check=True)'
change_robust: Runtime availability is probed lazily (only when tool is called); add startup health check to Hermes plugin init
detection_method: python -c 'from cluxion_agentplugin_preprocessing.runner import runtime_available; assert runtime_available(), "cluxion-runtime not available"'

what_it_checks: psutil module is installed and functional (required fallback for guard daemon resource sampling)
failure_symptom: Guard resource sampling fails; ResourceSnapshot collection returns zero/invalid values; capacity_decision admission always deferred
likely_causes: psutil not installed (not in dependencies)
psutil module exists but extensions (.so files) corrupted or wrong architecture
System does not support required psutil calls (very rare)
fix_steps: Check installation: pip show psutil
Reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall psutil
Test import: python -c 'import psutil; print(psutil.virtual_memory())'
Verify cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing dependencies: pip show cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing | grep Requires
Check Python version: psutil requires Python 3.5+, verify python --version
change_robust: psutil is a core dependency; add to pyproject.toml requires list for all Python versions
detection_method: python -c 'import psutil; m = psutil.virtual_memory(); s = psutil.swap_memory(); c = psutil.cpu_percent(); assert m.total > 0 and isinstance(c, float), "psutil broken"'

what_it_checks: PyYAML module is installed and can parse config.yaml
failure_symptom: hermes_config tool fails when reading/writing config.yaml; plugin enable/disable operations crash
likely_causes: PyYAML not installed
PyYAML C extension (.so) corrupted
config.yaml has invalid YAML syntax (tabs instead of spaces, etc.)
fix_steps: Check installation: pip show PyYAML
Reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall PyYAML
Test import: python -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load("test: 1")'
Validate config.yaml: python -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open("~/.hermes/config.yaml"))'
Check YAML syntax: yamllint ~/.hermes/config.yaml (if yamllint available)
change_robust: PyYAML is in dependencies; version constraint is >=6.0; test config parsing in plugin tests
detection_method: python -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load("test: value"); print("OK")'

what_it_checks: Native module wheel is built with abi3 (stable ABI) tag compatible with current Python interpreter
failure_symptom: ImportError: cannot import name 'run' from 'cluxion_queue_native'; module loads but functions missing
likely_causes: Wheel downloaded/built for Python 3.11 but running 3.12 (or vice versa) and stable ABI not used
cluxion_queue/pyproject.toml does not use pyo3 abi3 feature
maturin build version mismatch (<1.7 or >=2.0)
Wheel built on macOS x86_64 but running on arm64 (M1/M2 Mac) or vice versa
fix_steps: Check Python version and SOABI: python -c 'import sysconfig; print(f"{sysconfig.get_python_version()} {sysconfig.get_config_var(\"SOABI\")}")'
Verify abi3 in Cargo.toml: grep 'abi3' rust/cluxion_queue/Cargo.toml
Rebuild the supported merged platform wheel: bash scripts/build_local_wheel.sh

Resolve exactly one absolute wheel: WHEEL="$(python3 -c 'from pathlib import Path; w=list(Path("dist-merged").glob("cluxion_agentplugin_preprocessing-*.whl")); assert len(w)==1, f"expected exactly one wheel, got {len(w)}"; print(w[0].resolve())')"

Reinstall: HERMES_PY="$HOME/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/python"; uv tool install --force "$WHEEL" && uv pip install --python "$HERMES_PY" --no-deps --reinstall "$WHEEL" && uv pip check --python "$HERMES_PY"
Check architecture: python -c 'import platform; print(platform.machine())'  (must match wheel filename)
Confirm import: python -c 'import cluxion_queue_native; from cluxion_queue_native import __backend__; assert __backend__ == "rust_native"'
change_robust: Verify abi3 feature in Cargo.toml remains pinned; test wheel upload includes both arm64 and x86_64 variants for macOS
detection_method: python -c 'import sysconfig; print(sysconfig.get_config_var("SOABI")); from cluxion_queue_native import __backend__; assert __backend__ == "rust_native"'

what_it_checks: fcntl module is available on POSIX systems (not Windows native); advisory file locking works
failure_symptom: Concurrent dispatch bundle updates cause JSON corruption or lost writes; multiple Hermes instances on same dispatch dir race
likely_causes: Running on Windows without WSL2 (fcntl is Unix-only)
NFS mount with broken fcntl support (returns success but doesn't actually lock)
SELinux/AppArmor denying file locks
In-memory filesystem (tmpfs) issues with lock granularity
fix_steps: Check if fcntl available: python -c 'import fcntl; print("OK")'
On Windows, use WSL2 or switch dispatch_dir to Windows path and accept lack of locking (document risk)
On NFS, verify mount options: mount | grep dispatch-dir-path | grep actimeo,timeo (good NFS cache settings)
Test lock manually: python -c 'import fcntl; open(\"/tmp/.test_lock\", \"a+b\") as f: fcntl.flock(f.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_EX); print(\"Locked\"); fcntl.flock(f.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_UN)'
If NFS broken, override dispatch to local SSD: CLUXION_PREPROCESS_DISPATCH_DIR=/mnt/local-nvme/dispatch cluxion-runtime ...
change_robust: Code gracefully skips locking on non-POSIX; document that NFS users need proper mount options; test fcntl availability in pre-flight checks
detection_method: python -c 'import sys; import os; from pathlib import Path; test_lock = Path("/tmp/.fcntl_test"); test_lock.touch(); import fcntl; f = open(test_lock, "a+b"); fcntl.flock(f.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_EX); fcntl.flock(f.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_UN); f.close(); test_lock.unlink()'

what_it_checks: Environment variables for queue store, dispatch dir, runtime venv are consistent across daemon lifecycle and do not conflict
failure_symptom: Work queued in one store but dispatch bundles in another; daemon reads from different directory than client; inconsistent state
likely_causes: CLUXION_QUEUE_STORE_DIR and CLUXION_PREPROCESS_DISPATCH_DIR both set but to incompatible paths
Guard daemon started with one CLUXION_QUEUE_STORE_DIR but client code calls with different value
Legacy env vars (HERMES_CLUXION_*) and new vars (CLUXION_*) both set, fallback logic picks wrong one
Parent shell sets env var but subprocess inherits different value (PATH modification)
fix_steps: Print all relevant env vars: env | grep CLUXION; env | grep HERMES_CLUXION
If using non-defaults, verify consistency: CLUXION_QUEUE_STORE_DIR must be used everywhere; don't mix with HERMES_CLUXION_*
Check daemon startup: ps aux | grep guard-daemon and check arguments
Unset legacy vars: unset HERMES_CLUXION_DISPATCH_DIR HERMES_CLUXION_RUNTIME_VENV (if using new vars)
Test consistency: python -c 'import os; os.environ["CLUXION_QUEUE_STORE_DIR"]="/custom"; from cluxion_runtime.resources.queue_bridge import default_store_dir; print(default_store_dir())'
For multi-process systems, document env setup in startup scripts; verify all processes export same vars
change_robust: Fallback logic prefers new CLUXION_* over legacy HERMES_CLUXION_*; add startup trace logging to show resolved paths
detection_method: python -c 'import os; from cluxion_runtime.resources import queue_bridge; from cluxion_runtime.core.dispatch_store import default_dispatch_dir; qstore = queue_bridge.default_store_dir(); dstore = default_dispatch_dir(); print(f"Queue: {qstore}"); print(f"Dispatch: {dstore}"); assert qstore.parent != dstore.parent or "queue" not in str(dstore), "Paths should not collide"'

what_it_checks: SQLite WAL (Write-Ahead Logging) mode works correctly for queue database; locks are not permanently held
failure_symptom: Queue operations deadlock or timeout; two Hermes instances trying to dequeue hit 'database is locked'; WAL files accumulate (.sqlite-wal, .sqlite-shm)
likely_causes: SQLite version <3.10 doesn't support WAL properly
WAL files on network filesystem (NFS) causing sync issues
Multiple concurrent dequeue operations without proper transaction isolation
Database corruption from power loss (WAL checkpoint failed)
fix_steps: Check SQLite version: python -c 'import sqlite3; print(sqlite3.sqlite_version)'
Test WAL mode: python -c 'import sqlite3; conn = sqlite3.connect(":memory:"); print(conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL").fetchone())'

Check queue database journal mode: python -c 'from cluxion_runtime.resources.py_queue import _open_db; from pathlib import Path; conn = _open_db(Path.home() / \".local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue\"); print(conn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode\").fetchone())'
Clean up WAL files if stuck: rm ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue/*.sqlite-wal ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue/*.sqlite-shm
If on NFS, disable WAL: in py_queue.py set journal_mode=TRUNCATE instead (slower but safer)
Verify database integrity: python -c 'import sqlite3; conn = sqlite3.connect(\"<dbpath>\"); result = conn.execute(\"PRAGMA integrity_check\").fetchone(); print(result)'
change_robust: Test WAL mode in py_queue tests; add database integrity check on startup; log journal_mode at init time
detection_method: python -c 'from pathlib import Path; from cluxion_runtime.resources.py_queue import _open_db; import tempfile; tmpdir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()); conn = _open_db(tmpdir); pragma_mode = conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode").fetchone()[0]; assert pragma_mode.upper() == "WAL", f"Expected WAL, got {pragma_mode}"; conn.close()'

what_it_checks: JSON serialization is deterministic (sort_keys=True) so checksums/fingerprints match across runs
failure_symptom: Dispatch bundle checksums don't match on re-read; context compression produces different output for identical input
likely_causes: Code path not using sort_keys=True when serializing bundles/context
Python dict iteration order difference (unlikely on 3.7+ but possible in edge cases)
Floating point precision differences in timestamps or metrics
fix_steps: Check all json.dumps calls: grep -r 'json.dumps' src/ --include='*.py' | grep -v sort_keys
Audit dispatch_store.py and context_compress.py for determinism
Test round-trip: python -c 'import json; d = {"z":1, "a":2}; s1 = json.dumps(d, sort_keys=True); d2 = json.loads(s1); s2 = json.dumps(d2, sort_keys=True); assert s1 == s2'
Add checksums to test fixtures to catch non-determinism early
change_robust: Enforce sort_keys=True in all queue/dispatch serialization; add docstring reminding of determinism requirement
detection_method: python -c 'import json; d = {"z": 1, "a": 2}; j1 = json.dumps(d, sort_keys=True); j2 = json.dumps(d, sort_keys=True); assert j1 == j2, f"{j1} != {j2}"'

what_it_checks: Hermes deliver=agent source patch is absent, fully applied, or partially applied
failure_symptom: deliver=agent slash behavior silently does not exist or only some Hermes paths support it
likely_causes: Hermes source changed and patch anchors no longer match
A previous manual patch only touched some files
HERMES_AGENT_ROOT points at the wrong checkout
fix_steps: Run: cluxion-preprocess hermes-patch status
If needed, run: cluxion-preprocess hermes-patch apply
If anchors mismatch, restore or update the Hermes checkout before applying
change_robust: Patch is explicit opt-in and reports applied/partial/no-hermes/anchors-mismatch instead of silently editing on session start

what_it_checks: pyproject, plugin.yaml, package plugin.yaml, and Codex/Claude plugin manifests use the same version
failure_symptom: Marketplace plugin metadata, Hermes adapter metadata, and installed Python distribution report different versions
likely_causes: Version bumped in pyproject only
Root plugin artifact added without updating package metadata
fix_steps: Set every plugin manifest version to pyproject.toml project.version
change_robust: Doctor compares repo metadata files directly and fails on any drift

what_it_checks: Temporary files created during atomic writes (dispatch store, py_queue) are cleaned up on failure or success
failure_symptom: Dispatch dir fills with tmp* files on repeated failures; disk usage grows unbounded; tmpfs fills up on systems with small /tmp
likely_causes: NamedTemporaryFile exceptions not caught; temp file not cleaned up on write failure
os.fsync() call fails but exception not handled
path.replace() (atomic rename) fails, leaving temp file orphaned
Power loss during atomic write leaves tmpfile
fix_steps: Check dispatch dir for temp files: ls -la ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue/dispatch/ | grep tmp
Remove stale temps: find ~/.local/share/cluxion-agentplugin-preprocessing/queue/dispatch -name 'tmp*' -mtime +1 -delete
Review _atomic_write_json and py_queue._write_atomic for proper exception handling
Test failure mode: python -c 'import os; os.chmod(dispatch_dir, 0o444); try: _atomic_write_json(...); except: pass; print(list(dispatch_dir.glob(\"tmp*\")))'
Add cleanup context manager: ensure tempfile.unlink() on exception
change_robust: All atomic write calls must use try/finally or context manager for cleanup; add integration test for write failure scenarios
detection_method: python -c 'from pathlib import Path; import tempfile; tmpdir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()); from cluxion_runtime.core.dispatch_store import _atomic_write_json; test_path = tmpdir / \"test.json\"; _atomic_write_json(test_path, {\"test\": 1}); temp_files = list(tmpdir.glob(\"tmp*\")); assert len(temp_files) == 0, f\"Leftover temps: {temp_files}\"'
